description: Trigger-accuracy and behavioural evals. Trigger evals check whether the skill should fire on given prompts. Behavioural evals reference the inline ## Tests section of SKILL.md (20 acceptance test cases). The skill is a markdown procedure — a behavioural eval 'passes' when the A1-A9 protocol walked against the input yields the documented verdict.

evals.prompt: Audit this skill — has /map-feedback-loops drifted from what it was originally built to do?
expectations: Skill runs the MVI gate (artefact_path, artefact_type, repo_context, default_action)
Skill runs the A1-A9 deterministic protocol over the 6-axis rubric
Skill returns a keep / refactor / deprecate artefact_verdict derived from the axis scores
Skill emits a counterfactual against the operator's default_action

evals.prompt: Before we propagate this rule to the client repos, is it actually grounded in first principles?
expectations: Skill classifies the input as a single Claude Code artefact (in jurisdiction)
Skill runs the 6-axis rubric, trigger-gating which composed primitives fire
Skill emits a propagation_flag (repo_local / upstream / not_propagated)

evals.prompt: Has this skill's stated purpose drifted from a real, decision-relevant question?
expectations: Skill recognises this as an artefact-grounding audit (axis 1 + axis 2 territory), NOT a standalone framing question about a live decision
Skill runs the full artefact audit — it does NOT defer to /reduce-to-first-principles, because the subject is a standing artefact, not a decision in flight
this pins the discrimination boundary against trigger-005 (a genuine framing question DOES defer)

evals.prompt: Review the TypeScript in usePropertyEnriched.ts for bugs.
expectations: Skill recognises this as executable code, not a Claude Code artefact
Skill returns SCOPE_REFUSAL and redirects to /code-council

evals.prompt: Audit every skill in the skills directory at once.
expectations: Skill recognises this as a whole-repo request
Skill returns SCOPE_REFUSAL and redirects: audit one artefact per invocation, loop per-artefact

evals.prompt: Is 'adopt or build native' even the right question to be asking here?
expectations: Skill recognises this as a framing question about a live decision, not an artefact-grounding audit
Skill defers to /reduce-to-first-principles — no need to wrap it in an artefact audit

evals.prompt: Audit this rule. artefact_path: a well-formed rule file. artefact_type: rule. repo_context: workshop. operator_verdict not supplied. default_action: propagate this rule to the client repos without a grounding check.
expectations: verdict == PASS
all 6 axes score pass or not_applicable
artefact_verdict == keep
counterfactual.difference in {substantial, transformative} — verified grounding before a multi-repo propagation vs propagating blind
falsifier present with all three sub-fields (HIGH confidence)
propagation_flag set
anti_anchoring.hypothesis_provenance == not_applicable; anti_anchoring.verdict == not_applicable; anti_anchoring.independent_artefact_verdict is populated

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact (deletion test fails — duplicates another rule fully). operator_verdict: keep. hypothesis_provenance: operator. default_action supplied.
expectations: the 6-axis rubric runs independently before any compare
axis 3 (deletion test) scores fail → independent_artefact_verdict == deprecate
anti_anchoring.verdict == DISAGREED
artefact_verdict == deprecate (the independent verdict, not the operator's keep)
the operator's keep is recorded in anti_anchoring.operator_verdict; no silent adoption

evals.prompt: Audit this. artefact_path: a rule .md file. artefact_type: skill. repo_context and default_action supplied.
expectations: verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT
mvi.passed == false; mvi.missing names the artefact_type-vs-file mismatch
no fabricated artefact_verdict; artefact_verdict == not_evaluated
example input template emitted

evals.prompt: (skill invoked with no artefact_path)
expectations: verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT
mvi.missing includes artefact_path
output contains an example_input block
artefact_verdict == not_evaluated; no fabrication

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact. artefact_path: a .ts file. artefact_type: skill.
expectations: verdict == SCOPE_REFUSAL
scope.in_jurisdiction == false; scope.redirect names /code-council
no audit block produced; artefact_verdict == not_evaluated

evals.prompt: Audit this clean, well-formed skill. artefact_type: skill. operator_verdict not supplied. default_action: keep using it, it's fine.
expectations: all 6 axes score pass
artefact_verdict == keep
counterfactual.difference in {none, marginal} — the verdict matches the operator's default and nothing material surfaced
verdict == ADVISORY_PENDING; advisory_pending_reasons includes the counterfactual-below-substantial reason
anti_anchoring.verdict == not_applicable; anti_anchoring.independent_artefact_verdict is populated

evals.prompt: Audit this freight-dispatch depot-handover rule. artefact_type: rule. repo_context: client. default_action supplied.
expectations: verdict in {PASS, ADVISORY_PENDING}
every axis_scores[*].score is one of pass / weak / fail / not_applicable
artefact_verdict is one of keep / refactor / deprecate reached from the freight rule's own axis scores
axis_scores[*].cited_reasoning contains no string from the leak-set {CV.1, Wave 3a, synthesis-programme, ARV, MAO, real-estate} — vocabulary stays generic

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact. operator_verdict is the output of an upstream /skill-auditor-merger invocation. hypothesis_provenance: prior_skill. source_hypothesis populated with upstream_skill and upstream_invocation_id. default_action supplied.
expectations: A3.2 Branch B (validate-upstream) fires; the re-derivation runs under an alternative frame (artefact_type first)
anti_anchoring.verdict in {validate_upstream_stable, validate_upstream_unstable}
anti_anchoring.reasoning_chain contains a step naming the alternative frame used
verdict is NEVER AGREED/DISAGREED/INCONCLUSIVE for prior_skill provenance (AAP6)
if validate_upstream_unstable, verdict == ADVISORY_PENDING and artefact_verdict == not_evaluated, with independent_artefact_verdict still populated

evals.prompt: Audit this rule that duplicates content fully covered by another rule — deleting it causes nothing to reappear. artefact_type: rule. default_action: keep it.
expectations: axis 3 (deletion test) scores fail — no specific consumer can be named
artefact_verdict == deprecate (deprecate triggers on axis 3 fail)
cited_reasoning for axis 3 states no consumer could be named
recommendation.recommended_action names the deletion path

evals.prompt: Audit this skill whose procedure has partially drifted from its stated purpose but which has named consumers and is salvageable with a bounded edit. artefact_type: skill. default_action supplied.
expectations: axis 2 (procedure-purpose fidelity) scores fail or weak — the procedure drifted
axis 3 (deletion test) scores pass — the artefact earns its keep
the fix is bounded-edit-class (core mechanism sound), not rewrite-class → not deprecate
artefact_verdict == refactor; recommendation.refactor_detail names the specific bounded fix

evals.prompt: Audit this rule whose core procedure is centrally a comparison. axis-2 Primitive-1 reads the procedure as faithfully serving the purpose; axis-4 Primitive-3 reads the embedded comparison as rung-1 / incommensurable. artefact_type: rule. default_action supplied.
expectations: A5 detects the contradiction between Primitive 1 (axis 2) and Primitive 3 (axis 4) about the same property
composition_contradictions has >=1 entry with both assessment_a and assessment_b populated verbatim
the verdict is NOT averaged; A5 floors artefact_verdict at refactor
artefact_verdict in {refactor, deprecate} — never keep; neither primitive output is silently dropped (AAP4 guard)

evals.prompt: Audit this simple naming-convention rule that encodes no comparison and handles no operator pushback. artefact_type: rule. default_action supplied.
expectations: axis 4 both structural indicators absent → score not_applicable; trigger_fired == false
axis 4 cited_reasoning documents why neither indicator was present
the artefact is not penalised for the not_applicable axis
a clean artefact still reaches keep with axis 4 not_applicable

evals.prompt: Audit this rule that prescribes success-criteria discipline for other artefacts but carries no Success Criteria / verification block on itself. artefact_type: rule. default_action supplied.
expectations: axis 6 (verifiability) scores fail — the rule states no checkable success criteria for itself
cited_reasoning for axis 6 names the missing self-applied success criteria
artefact_verdict == refactor (a fail axis with the artefact still earning its keep → refactor)
recommendation.refactor_detail says add a Success Criteria / verification block to the rule itself

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact. audit_loop_depth: 2.
expectations: A1 recursion guard fires — audit_loop_depth > 1 is a runaway (AAP8)
verdict == SCOPE_REFUSAL; scope.redirect states the recursion-depth-exceeded reason
no audit block produced; artefact_verdict == not_evaluated
the skill halts before the rubric runs

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact. operator_verdict: keep. hypothesis_provenance: session_history. default_action supplied.
expectations: the operator-confirmation question fires (AskUserQuestion) BEFORE any compare — is the verdict yours or did Claude propose it earlier?

'Mine' routes to Branch A; 'Claude proposed it' routes to Branch B with a synthesised source_hypothesis (upstream_skill: claude_session), running the claude_session degraded-mode self-adversarial re-derivation
anti_anchoring.verdict is never silently not_applicable
unresolved ambiguity after one re-ask → verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT, artefact_verdict == not_evaluated, mvi.missing names the unresolved provenance, independent_artefact_verdict still populated

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact. artefact_path: an n8n workflow JSON. artefact_type: skill.
expectations: verdict == SCOPE_REFUSAL
scope.in_jurisdiction == false
scope.redirect contains 'out of jurisdiction' and does NOT name /code-council — distinct from the executable-code redirect
no audit block produced; artefact_verdict == not_evaluated

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact. audit_loop_depth: 1.
expectations: the recursion guard does NOT fire at depth 1 — depth 1 is the legitimate audit-the-skills-loop depth
verdict in {PASS, ADVISORY_PENDING} — NOT SCOPE_REFUSAL
the audit block is produced; the rubric runs normally
invocation_metadata.audit_loop_depth == 1

evals.prompt: Audit this artefact with no discernible stated purpose — its content is a mechanism with no end it serves. artefact_type: rule. default_action supplied.
expectations: mvi.passed == true — a missing stated purpose does NOT halt MVI; the skill proceeds to the audit
artefact.stated_purpose == null
axis 1 scores fail — Primitive 1 cannot extract a decision-relevant irreducible question (solution in search of a problem)
artefact_verdict == deprecate (deprecate triggers on axis 1 fail — the second of the three deprecate triggers)

evals.prompt: Audit this hook whose silent action drives a slow feedback loop that crowds out a better artefact over time. artefact_type: hook. default_action supplied.
expectations: the light axis-5 scan spots a non-obvious candidate adverse effect (depends on a multi-step chain)
axis 5 escalates to a /map-feedback-loops DECISION-mode invocation (decision = keep this artefact)
axis_scores[4].score == fail; axis_scores[4].composed_primitive == map-feedback-loops; trigger_fired == true
artefact_verdict in {refactor, deprecate}

evals.prompt: Audit this thin / near-stub artefact — well-formatted but little real content, no checkable success criteria, no nameable consumer. artefact_type: skill. default_action: keep it.
expectations: the skill does NOT rubber-stamp a HIGH keep (AAP2) — a near-empty artefact cannot earn HIGH confidence
confidence in {MEDIUM, LOW}
the verdict is NOT HIGH keep; artefact_verdict is derived strictly from the axis scores (AAP1 holds)
the skill does NOT manufacture a deprecate on a thin-but-grounded artefact (AAP3) — the verdict tracks the axis scores
